Let me give you the big picture.
Hi Emmanuele. Before Thursday, give me the big picture. How many patients are on the program?
We have many patients, approximately two hundred, on average.
Good. And how much access do we have in the private market?
There is a lot of demand, but only a little access. A large portion has no coverage.
I see. How many hospitals are ready for the launch?
Only a few are ready. The majority are still not ready.
Understood. And how much time do we have before the deadline?
We have a little time, a few weeks. The ratio of public to private is three to one.
We have many patients on the program.
How much access do we have?

Hi Emmanuele, it's David from the Global team. A quick brief before your field update on Thursday. We want the numbers, but keep them simple. First, how many patients are on the program right now? An average is fine. Second, how much access do we have in the public market, and how much in the private market? A few clear figures are better than a lot of detail. And third, how many hospitals are ready for the launch? If only a few are ready, that is important to know. Do not worry about perfect data. We want the big picture. See you Thursday.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Claire from the Global data team. I've looked at the Brazil dashboard, and I have a few comments. The majority of your patients are in the public system, which is great. But a large portion of the private market still has very little coverage. We have a lot of demand, but not many hospitals are ready yet. So my advice is simple. For Thursday, show the ratio of public to private, give one average for patient volume, and name a few priorities. That is all the Global team needs. Thank you, and good luck.

Hi David, here is a quick update on the numbers. We have many patients on the program. A lot of demand is in the private market. But we have only a few hospitals ready for the launch. And we still have a little time before the deadline.
